skill.name: react-server-components-framework
skill.description: Design React Server Components with Next.js App Router using server-first architecture, streaming, Server Actions, routing patterns, and migration guidance.
skill.summary: Build Next.js App Router applications with React Server Components, Server Actions, streaming, and practical migration patterns.
skill.author: AI Agent Hub

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, environment, and browser-storage patterns, but review found these are primarily markdown and TypeScript examples for Next.js development rather than hidden executable behavior. No prompt-injection text or confirmed malicious intent was found. The skill should publish with a warning because some copyable examples need stronger security caveats, especially stored HTML rendering and authorization checks for destructive actions.

medium_findings.title: Copyable Stored HTML Rendering Example
medium_findings.description: The blog example renders post content with dangerouslySetInnerHTML. In a real application, stored HTML must be sanitized before rendering or it can create stored cross-site scripting risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly maps stored post.content into dangerouslySetInnerHTML. The file is an educational example, so malicious intent is not confirmed, but the copy-paste security risk is clear.
medium_findings.remediation: Add explicit sanitization guidance and show a safe renderer or trusted-content boundary before using dangerouslySetInnerHTML.

medium_findings.title: Destructive Server Actions Lack Authorization in Template
medium_findings.description: The delete and bulk update Server Action examples mutate records by id without the same session and ownership checks shown in the update example. Copied as-is, this can teach insecure authorization patterns.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The mutation examples are concrete and lack visible authorization checks in the cited blocks. They are templates, not active marketplace code, so the risk is educational rather than direct compromise.
medium_findings.remediation: Mirror the ownership checks used in updateResource for deleteResource, toggleComplete, and bulkUpdateResources.

low_findings.title: Static Command Findings Are Markdown and Framework Syntax
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution findings are mostly inline markdown code spans, JSX template literals, and framework snippets. They are not invoked by install scripts or runtime automation in this skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation bullets and fenced examples. I did not find evidence that the skill executes shell commands from these strings.
low_findings.remediation: No code change is required for execution risk, but clearer labels for examples can reduce scanner noise.

low_findings.title: Environment and Network Examples Use Placeholder Endpoints
low_findings.description: The environment-variable and fetch findings occur in server-component examples that show how to call an API with a server-side secret. This is a legitimate framework pattern, but users should avoid logging or returning secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The example intentionally demonstrates server-only secret use with api.example.com. It is not exfiltration, but it is security-sensitive guidance that should remain clearly server-only.
low_findings.remediation: Keep the server-only warning and add a note not to pass secret values to Client Components or rendered props.

low_findings.title: Browser Storage Example Parses Local Data
low_findings.description: The Client Component template reads localStorage and parses JSON. This is normal client-side behavior, but copied code should handle parse failures and avoid storing sensitive data in browser storage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The browser API usage is explicit and limited to an example. The main concern is missing defensive parsing and storage guidance, not malicious data collection.
low_findings.remediation: Wrap JSON.parse in error handling and document that secrets should not be stored in localStorage.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Dangerous Combination Dismissed After Context Review
dangerous_patterns.description: The scanner combined code execution, network, and credential-access indicators into a critical heuristic. Context review found these indicators distributed across documentation and templates, with no hidden script, installer, or exfiltration flow tying them together.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The underlying patterns exist, but they appear in educational examples and framework documentation. I did not find semantic evidence of a combined malicious behavior chain.
dangerous_patterns.remediation: Treat the skill as medium risk due to copyable insecure examples, not as blocked malware.

content.value_statement: React Server Components change where data, rendering, and interactivity belong in a Next.js application. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical patterns for App Router architecture, Server Actions, streaming, and migration work.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use Server Components or Client Components in Next.js App Router projects.
Provides templates for Server Components, Client Components, and Server Actions.
Documents data fetching strategies with caching, revalidation, and route segment settings.
Shows streaming patterns with Suspense and loading states.
Covers advanced routing patterns, including parallel routes and intercepting routes.
Includes migration guidance from the Pages Router to the App Router.
limitations: It is guidance and template content, not a runnable framework package.
Examples use placeholder imports such as database and authentication helpers.
It focuses on Next.js App Router patterns and does not cover every React framework.
Some examples need project-specific security hardening before production use.

use_cases.title: Plan an App Router Architecture
use_cases.description: Map routes, layouts, data boundaries, and interactive islands before building a new Next.js application.

use_cases.title: Implement Server Actions Safely
use_cases.description: Create mutation flows with validation, revalidation, redirects, and clear authorization checkpoints.

use_cases.title: Migrate Pages Router Features
use_cases.description: Convert data fetching, metadata, layouts, and API route patterns into App Router equivalents.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Component Boundaries
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Next.js page idea and decide which parts should be Server Components and which parts should be Client Components. Explain each boundary briefly.

prompt_templates.title: Design Data Fetching
prompt_templates.prompt: Design the data fetching strategy for this App Router route. Include caching mode, revalidation, loading UI, and error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Create Server Actions
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Server Action plan for this form. Include validation, authorization, mutation steps, cache revalidation, and user-facing error states.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an RSC Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Pages Router feature for migration to the App Router. Identify required route, layout, metadata, data fetching, and client boundary changes.

output_examples.input: A product dashboard needs filters, charts, and secure account data.
output_examples.output: A boundary plan that keeps account data and chart queries on the server, moves filters into a small Client Component, and streams slow chart sections behind Suspense.

output_examples.input: A blog editor needs a publish action and route refresh.
output_examples.output: A Server Action checklist covering schema validation, session checks, ownership checks, database update, path revalidation, redirect behavior, and error messages.

output_examples.input: A Pages Router article page uses getStaticProps and Head.
output_examples.output: A migration outline that replaces static props with an async Server Component, moves metadata into generateMetadata, and preserves revalidation behavior.

best_practices: Keep Client Components small and place them near the interactive UI they control.
Validate and authorize every Server Action before mutating data.
Use Suspense boundaries around slow sections so the page can stream useful content early.
anti_patterns: Do not mark entire pages as Client Components when only a small widget needs interactivity.
Do not pass secrets or server-only objects into Client Component props.
Do not copy examples into production without adding project-specific validation and authorization.

faq.question: Does this skill install a React framework?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance, templates, examples, and checklists for building with React Server Components and Next.js.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it only for Next.js?
faq.answer: Most guidance targets Next.js App Router because that is the main supported React Server Components workflow.

faq.question: Can it help with existing Pages Router projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes migration guidance for data fetching, layouts, metadata, and route structure.

faq.question: Does it cover Server Actions?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes Server Action patterns for forms, validation, revalidation, uploads, and optimistic updates.

faq.question: Is the content production-ready as written?
faq.answer: No. The examples are starting points and need application-specific authentication, authorization, validation, and sanitization.
